post.description:
Validate a source configuration and return a preview.

Drives Step 3 (Scan) of the ingestion wizard (#208). Persists nothing:
no ``WikiRecord``, no invocation registration, no token write. The
response carries reachability + a connector-specific preview shape.

All three source types are supported: ``git``, ``confluence``, ``jira``.

Request shape: ``source_type`` is **required** — it discriminates the
typed scope/auth variants (#215). Callers must include it explicitly;
omitting it returns 422 (Pydantic ``union_tag_not_found``). Pre-#215
callers that relied on the implicit ``"git"`` default need to update.

Errors (no ``responses=`` declaration intentionally — the 400 body
doesn't match :class:`ErrorResponse`'s top-level shape and the SPA
codegen would otherwise produce wrong client types.):

* ``400`` — ``{"detail": {"error": <message>, "reachable": <bool>}}``
  for unreachable / auth-fail / invalid-scope / invalid-JQL.
* ``422`` — Pydantic validation error: ``source_type`` missing or not
  ``"git"`` / ``"confluence"`` / ``"jira"``, or per-variant field
  validation failed.

TODO(follow-up): rate-limit / tmpdir-exhaustion guard. A malicious
authenticated user can fan out concurrent scans; each shallow clone
creates a tmpdir for the context-manager lifetime. Belongs at the
gateway / network policy layer rather than in this handler.

get.description: Get wiki detail with pages and their content.

schema.title: Response Get Wiki Api V1 Wikis  Wiki Id  Get

get.description: Full-text search over wiki pages with graph-expansion re-ranking.
List all pages in a wiki with their titles and descriptions.
Return the wikilink graph neighborhood for a single wiki page.
Return the full content of a single wiki page identified by its title.
Get a single wiki page content. page_id can be section/page format.

schema.title: Response Get Wiki Page Api V1 Wikis  Wiki Id  Pages  Page Id  Get

get.description: Paginated Q&A history for a wiki.

post.description:
Diff a fresh-parse payload against the indexed snapshot.

The caller supplies the freshly parsed nodes (id + content_hash + path);
the server compares them against the wiki's stored ``repo_nodes`` and
returns the resulting change set plus the wiki pages those changes
touch (via the ``page_symbols`` reverse index from #116 PR 1).

Read-only: no parsing, no LLM, no writes. PR 3 will wire the actual
re-parse loop on top of this primitive.

Trigger an incremental refresh on a previously-generated wiki.

The caller supplies pre-parsed nodes (same shape as ``/diff``).
The server diffs them against the indexed snapshot, dispatches each
affected page through the trivial / edit / structural regimes, and
streams progress over SSE.

Owner-only — same access model as ``/refresh`` since this surfaces
internal content_hash + node_id state through the per-page events.

patch.description: Change a wiki's visibility between 'personal' and 'shared'.
Update the user-provided description for a wiki.

schema.title: Response Update Wiki Description Api V1 Wikis  Wiki Id  Description Patch

get.description:
Export a wiki as a downloadable ZIP archive.

Supports two formats:
- ``obsidian`` — Obsidian vault layout with YAML frontmatter
- ``wikis`` — Wikis-to-Wikis bundle for re-importing into another instance

post.description: Import a wiki from a ``.wikiexport`` bundle produced by the wikis export.

Stream a project-level recompute (relatedness → cross-repo → leiden).

Gated server-side by ``flags.project_graph``. Emits SSE progress events
matching the wiki-generation widget contract.

get.description: Full-text search over all wiki pages in a project with graph-expansion re-ranking.

post.description:
Build a code-map for all wikis in a project.

Proxies to the codemap pipeline with ``project_id`` set and
``research_type=codemap``.

AffectedPageResponse.description: One wiki page touched by the change set.

AskRequest.description: Request to ask a question about a wiki.
AskRequest.title: AskRequest

AtlassianAuth.title: AtlassianAuth
AtlassianAuth.description:
Auth for Confluence or Jira — OAuth 2.0 access token OR API-token basic auth.

Atlassian Cloud accepts two authentication shapes:

1. **OAuth 2.0** — ``access_token`` (with optional ``refresh_token`` +
   ``client_id`` for automatic refresh).  This is what the in-app
   "Connect to Atlassian" flow produces.
2. **API-token Basic auth** — ``email`` + ``api_token``, the shape
   expected by clients like ``atlassian-python-api``.  Users generate
   API tokens at https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens
   and supply their account email; the backend sends an HTTP Basic
   ``Authorization: Basic base64(email:api_token)`` header.

Exactly one shape must be supplied (validated below).  ``extra="forbid"``
keeps GitAuth fields (e.g. ``pat``) from sneaking in when the source is
Atlassian.

CallStack.description: A named call-flow chain, e.g. 'Frontend Auth' or 'MCP Auth'.
CallStack.title: CallStack

CallStackStep.description: One step in a call stack chain.
CallStackStep.title: CallStackStep

ChangeSetResponse.description: Aggregate change set — one entry per kind.

CodeMapData.description: Hierarchical call-tree for the code-map right panel.
CodeMapData.title: CodeMapData

CodeMapSection.description: A top-level section in the call-tree (usually one source file).
CodeMapSection.title: CodeMapSection

CodeMapSymbol.description: A single symbol (function / class / variable) shown as a leaf in the tree.
CodeMapSymbol.title: CodeMapSymbol

ConfluenceScanPreview.title: ConfluenceScanPreview
ConfluenceScanPreview.description:
Preview returned for ``source_type == "confluence"``.

``page_count`` on each space is the total returned by the Confluence API
for that space.  When the API paginates without a total-results field,
``page_count`` is left as ``None`` and the caller should render it as
``"?"``.

ConfluenceScanRequest.title: ConfluenceScanRequest
ConfluenceScanRequest.description: Scan request for a Confluence instance.

DiffWikiRequest.title: DiffWikiRequest
DiffWikiRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /wikis/{wiki_id}/diff`` — supply the freshly parsed
nodes; the server diffs them against the indexed snapshot.

Capped at 50 000 nodes per request to bound the cost of the per-node
reverse-index lookup. The largest repos in our corpus parse to ~30k
architectural nodes; 50k gives ~1.5x headroom before PR 3 swaps in the
batched ``get_pages_citing_nodes`` query.

DiffWikiResponse.title: DiffWikiResponse
DiffWikiResponse.description: Result of the diff endpoint — no writes occur.

GenerateWikiRequest.description:
Request to generate a wiki from a repository or Atlassian source.

Preferred (new) shape
---------------------
source_type + scope + auth — see module docstring for field shapes.

Backwards-compatible (legacy) shape
------------------------------------
repo_url + branch + access_token — auto-normalized into the new shape
by the ``_normalize_legacy`` validator so old callers keep working.
GenerateWikiRequest.title: GenerateWikiRequest

GitAuth.title: GitAuth
GitAuth.description:
Auth for a git source (optional PAT).

``extra="forbid"`` ensures that AtlassianAuth fields (e.g. ``access_token``)
are rejected when paired with ``source_type="git"``, making the discriminated
union enforce valid combos at validation time.

GitScanRequest.title: GitScanRequest
GitScanRequest.description: Scan request for a git repository.

IncrementalRefreshRequest.title: IncrementalRefreshRequest
IncrementalRefreshRequest.description:
POST ``/wikis/{id}/incremental-refresh`` body.

Caller supplies pre-parsed nodes (same shape as ``/diff``). Capped at
50_000 nodes per request to bound the orchestrator's snapshot work.

IncrementalRefreshResponse.title: IncrementalRefreshResponse
IncrementalRefreshResponse.description:
Initial response — actual progress flows over SSE.

The orchestrator runs asynchronously after this returns; subscribe
to ``/invocations/{invocation_id}/stream`` for ``page_unchanged``,
``page_patched``, ``page_edited``, ``page_regenerated``, and the
final ``incremental_summary`` event with the run stats.

Invocation.title: Invocation
Invocation.description: Tracks the state of a background wiki generation job.

JiraScanRequest.title: JiraScanRequest
JiraScanRequest.description: Scan request for a Jira instance.

NodeChangeResponse.title: NodeChangeResponse
NodeChangeResponse.description: One change in the response. Field population depends on ``kind``.

PageListItem.description: A brief description of a single wiki page, used in list responses.

PageNeighbor.title: PageNeighbor
PageNeighbor.description: A neighboring wiki page with its relationship direction.

PageNeighborsResponse.description: Response containing the neighbor graph for a single wiki page.

ParsedNodeInput.title: ParsedNodeInput
ParsedNodeInput.description:
One node from a fresh parse, supplied to the diff endpoint.

Only the three fields needed for change classification are required;
callers can pass the same dicts they would otherwise hand to
``upsert_nodes_batch`` (extra keys are ignored).
